repository_intake_record.name: shiro
repository_intake_record.selection_reason: Listed in TRACE4QR WP2 seed set (MD §5). Apache Shiro is an application security framework providing authentication, authorization, cryptographic services, session security, credential hashing, certificate handling, and enterprise security workflow scenarios. High-density cryptographic usage: dedicated crypto module with cipher, hash, and KDF sub-modules (AES/GCM, SHA-256/384/512, BCrypt, Argon2). Satisfies all eligibility criteria: Apache-2.0 license, pinnable commit SHA, meaningful Java source, improves cryptographic pattern coverage.
repository_intake_record.review_notes: sha_source: local_git — execution_mode: operator_grep. Grep sweep executed locally via PowerShell Select-String on the cloned repository at C:\Users\Admin\Desktop\trino\apache_shiro\shiro. 421 unique file paths returned. All paths converted to repo-relative format.

file_inventory._inventory_note: All 420 grep-hit files categorized. Rules: src/main/java → source_files; src/test/java|src/test/resources|*Test.java|*IT.java → test_files; pom.xml → dependency_manifests; *.xml|*.yml|*.yaml|*.properties|*.json|features.xml (non-pom, non-source) → config_files. Total: 420 files across all categories.

preliminary_call_sites.call: DefaultBlockCipherService constructor with ALGORITHM_NAME="AES", default key size 128 bits, mode GCM
javax.crypto.Cipher.getInstance(), SecretKeySpec, IvParameterSpec, GCMParameterSpec — JCA symmetric cipher operations with auto-generated IV prepended to ciphertext
MessageDigest.getInstance(algorithmName) — pluggable JCA hash; default algorithm SHA-256 in Hasher CLI
OpenBSDBCrypt.generate() / OpenBSDBCrypt.checkPassword() via BouncyCastle; DEFAULT_ALGORITHM_NAME="bcrypt2y", DEFAULT_COST=10, SALT_LENGTH=16
Argon2BytesGenerator.generate() via BouncyCastle; default variant argon2id (referenced in DefaultPasswordService.DEFAULT_HASH_ALGORITHM)
new SecureRandom(); secureRandom.nextBytes() — default 16 bytes (128 bits)
AesCipherService.encrypt() / decrypt() for RememberMe principal serialization; setCipherKey() API for operator-supplied key
DefaultHashService with DEFAULT_HASH_ALGORITHM="argon2id"; Shiro1CryptFormat / Shiro2CryptFormat for output encoding
CipherKeySupplier.get() returns hardcoded hex string "34D7E5C61B87A38C971B3716AED7899E" — hardcoded AES cipher key in test/integration fixture

DefaultPasswordService, DefaultHashService, SecureRandomNumberGenerator, Shiro2CryptFormat — CLI hash utility; DEFAULT_ALGORITHM_NAME="SHA-256", DEFAULT_PASSWORD_ALGORITHM_NAME from DefaultPasswordService, DEFAULT_PASSWORD_NUM_ITERATIONS=350000

confirmed_crypto_signals.description: AesCipherService extends DefaultBlockCipherService with ALGORITHM_NAME="AES", default key 128 bits, mode overridden from CBC to GCM. javax.crypto.spec.GCMParameterSpec imported. Used by AbstractRememberMeManager for RememberMe cookie encryption.
BlowfishCipherService extends DefaultBlockCipherService. Present as a supported but non-default cipher option.

JcaCipherService abstract base: javax.crypto.CipherInputStream, SecretKeySpec, IvParameterSpec, SecureRandom. Auto-generates IV (128 bits default), prepends it to ciphertext. Pluggable operation modes and padding schemes via OperationMode and PaddingScheme enums.

Dedicated hash classes Sha256Hash, Sha384Hash, Sha512Hash extend SimpleHash which calls MessageDigest.getInstance(algorithmName) via JCA. Corresponding credentials matchers Sha256CredentialsMatcher, Sha384CredentialsMatcher, Sha512CredentialsMatcher present.

BCryptHash imports org.bouncycastle.crypto.generators.OpenBSDBCrypt. DEFAULT_ALGORITHM_NAME="bcrypt2y", DEFAULT_COST=10, SALT_LENGTH=16. Supported variants: bcrypt2, bcrypt2a, bcrypt2b, bcrypt2y. SecureRandom used for salt generation.

Argon2Hash imports org.bouncycastle.crypto.generators.Argon2BytesGenerator and Argon2Parameters. Default variant argon2id confirmed in DefaultPasswordService.DEFAULT_HASH_ALGORITHM="argon2id". Parameters follow IETF draft-irtf-cfrg-argon2-13 memory-constrained defaults. SecureRandom used for salt.
SecureRandomNumberGenerator wraps java.security.SecureRandom. Default output 16 bytes (128 bits). Used throughout crypto module for salt and key generation.

AbstractRememberMeManager uses AesCipherService to encrypt/decrypt serialized PrincipalCollection for RememberMe cookies. setCipherKey() API allows operator-supplied symmetric key. Default key auto-generated at startup — Javadoc explicitly warns that the default key is insecure for production.

CipherKeyGenerator.get() returns hardcoded hex string "34D7E5C61B87A38C971B3716AED7899E" as AES cipher key via CipherKeySupplier CDI annotation. Located in integration-test fixture. This is test/integration key material — not production code, but represents a real hardcoded key pattern.

SslFilter (web and jakarta-ee) enforces HTTPS for configured URL paths. Supports configurable port (default 443), HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) opt-in with maxAge=31536000 and includeSubDomains option. Delegates to request.isSecure().

JndiLdapContextFactory supports authenticationMechanism property (example in Javadoc: DIGEST-MD5). Connects to LDAP via JNDI. Authentication mechanism is runtime-configurable — specific algorithm not hardcoded in source.
DefaultPasswordService is the main password hashing facade. DEFAULT_HASH_ALGORITHM="argon2id". Supports Shiro1CryptFormat and Shiro2CryptFormat (modular crypt format). Pluggable HashService backend.

Hasher CLI tool wraps DefaultPasswordService, DefaultHashService, SecureRandomNumberGenerator. DEFAULT_ALGORITHM_NAME="SHA-256", DEFAULT_PASSWORD_NUM_ITERATIONS=350000. Outputs Shiro2CryptFormat by default for passwords.

BouncyCastle declared as compile dependency in root pom.xml at version 1.84 (<bouncycastle.version>1.84</bouncycastle.version>). Used by BCryptHash (OpenBSDBCrypt) and Argon2Hash (Argon2BytesGenerator, Argon2Parameters).

BearerToken class and BearerHttpAuthenticationFilter implement bearer token authentication flow. Token value carried as credential — actual token signature/validation algorithm not visible in this layer.

suspected_false_positives.reason: Matched 'key' keyword due to Map key/value semantics (java.util.Map). No cryptographic operation present.
Class name contains 'Hash' but refers to java.util.HashMap used as a cache backend, not a cryptographic hash function.
Matched 'key' keyword in cache entry key/value API. No cryptographic usage.
Matched 'sign' or 'key' as part of permission/authorization vocabulary (e.g., permission signing meaning 'assigning'). No cryptographic signature operation.
Matched 'key' in Quartz job/scheduler configuration key names. No cryptographic usage.
Matched 'key' or 'sign' in event bus subscriber registration patterns. No cryptographic operation.

ingress_boundaries.description: Authentication ingress: UsernamePasswordToken and BearerToken carry credentials inbound. HashedCredentialsMatcher / PasswordMatcher compares submitted credential against stored hash at this boundary.
RememberMe cookie ingress: CookieRememberMeManager reads AES-GCM encrypted cookie, decrypts via AbstractRememberMeManager, deserializes PrincipalCollection.
SSL enforcement ingress: SslFilter validates that incoming HTTP requests are over TLS (request.isSecure(), port 443) before permitting access.

egress_boundaries.description: RememberMe cookie egress: AbstractRememberMeManager serializes and AES-GCM encrypts PrincipalCollection before writing to cookie. CookieRememberMeManager sets Base64-encoded ciphertext as cookie value.
Password hash egress: DefaultPasswordService / HashedCredentialsMatcher outputs Shiro1CryptFormat or Shiro2CryptFormat encoded hash strings for storage in credential stores (DB, properties file).

transport_security_layers.description: SslFilter enforces HTTPS at the web filter layer. HSTS header support (opt-in). No direct TLS configuration (cipher suites, protocol versions) visible in source — delegated to the servlet container (Jetty/Tomcat).

LDAP over TLS: JndiLdapContextFactory configures JNDI LDAP connections. TLS/SSL and authentication mechanism (e.g., DIGEST-MD5) configurable via authenticationMechanism and environment properties. Actual TLS details delegated to JNDI provider.

storage_security_layers.description: Credential storage layer: Passwords are hashed with argon2id (default), bcrypt, or SHA-2 family before storage. Shiro1CryptFormat / Shiro2CryptFormat used as wire format encoding (modular crypt format).
Session identity storage: RememberMe principal serialized and AES-GCM encrypted before cookie persistence.

certificate_trust_boundaries.description: No keystore, truststore, or certificate validation code observed in grep hits. TLS certificate validation delegated entirely to servlet container and JNDI provider. needs_follow_up: verify absence of certificate pinning or custom TrustManager in support modules not fully scanned.

dependency_boundaries.description: BouncyCastle 1.84 (org.bouncycastle) — used for BCrypt (OpenBSDBCrypt) and Argon2 (Argon2BytesGenerator). Declared in root pom.xml.

Java Cryptography Architecture (JCA/JCE) — used directly for AES, Blowfish, SHA-2, SecureRandom via javax.crypto and java.security packages. No explicit JCE provider override observed; defaults to JDK security provider.
